title: BOM — NAICS 221122 Electric Power Distribution (Hardware Target)

sections.title: Applicability Note
sections.content:
| Question | Answer |
|---|---|
| Is the NAICS output a physical product? | **Partly** — the service is electricity delivery, but the delivery is performed by a durable capital asset (substations + feeders + controls) with a real compositional structure. |
| Does Mein Solar's product end up IN a physical deliverable? | **YES** — the BESS asset is integrated into the DSO grid and remains there. |
| BOM vs VN test | BESS is IN the grid (BOM), not making the grid (not VN). |
| Conclusion | BOM applies to the **grid capital asset**. The "service output" (electrons) sits above the BOM. |

---

sections.title: B.1 — L4 Subsystems of the Distribution System
sections.content:
Eight L4 subsystems identified. Functional-not-form names.

| # | L4 Subsystem | Function | Present in every grid? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Primary Substation (HV/MV step-down) | Receive HV from transmission, step down to MV, switch/protect | YES |
| 2 | MV Distribution Feeder Network | Transport MV energy from primary substation to secondary substations | YES |
| 3 | Secondary Substation (MV/LV step-down) | Step down MV to LV for end customers | YES (except direct-MV customers) |
| 4 | LV Distribution Network | Deliver LV energy to final consumer connection | YES |
| 5 | Protection, Automation & Control (PAC) | Fault detection, isolation, restoration, SCADA, DMS | YES |
| 6 | Metering & Customer Interface (AMI) | Meter energy at each delivery point, report to DSO | YES |
| 7 | **Grid-Support Storage (Netzbooster BESS)** ← **Mein Solar position** | Active constraint relief, voltage support, substation-upgrade deferral | Growing — NOT yet on every grid |
| 8 | DER & Prosumer Interconnection Interface | Connect PV/wind/storage/EV assets owned by third parties | Growing |

---

sections.title: B.2 — Full L4 → L0 Decomposition with Alternatives
sections.content:
Alternatives tables at every level transition. Market shares are **EU-weighted** unless noted.

### L4 #1 — Primary Substation (HV/MV)

**L4 → L3 alternatives (substation architecture)**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Outdoor air-insulated switchyard (AIS) | 60% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Siemens Energy / ABB product docs, EU-weighted |
| Gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) indoor | 30% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Mordor Intelligence GIS 2025 |
| Hybrid (AIS + GIS modules) | 10% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Analyst estimate |

**L3 modules (each primary substation):** HV bay module · Power transformer module · MV switchgear module · Auxiliary module · Control house module → all five mandatory.

**L3 → L2 — Power transformer module**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Oil-immersed (mineral) | 65% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| Ester-fluid (natural/synthetic) | 20% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Grand View Research |
| Dry-type (cast-resin / air) | 14% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| SF6-gas-filled | 1% | → Stable | LOW | Niche |

**L2 → L1 (transformer core+winding assembly)**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Grain-oriented electrical steel (GOES) core + copper winding | 70% | → Stable | HIGH | Fortune Business Insights |
| GOES core + aluminum winding | 20% | ↑ Growing (cost) | MEDIUM | Analyst estimate |
| Amorphous-metal core + copper winding | 10% | ↑ Growing (efficiency regs) | MEDIUM | EcoDesign EU 2019/2021 |

**L1 → L0**

| L1 Part | L0 Raw Material | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Core laminations | GOES silicon steel (3%Si) | 85% | → Stable | HIGH | ISO 6316 |
| Core laminations (premium) | Amorphous metal ribbon (Metglas) | 15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Industry |
| Winding conductor | Electrolytic copper (Cu-ETP) | 75% | → Stable | HIGH | DIN EN 1976 |
| Winding conductor | Aluminum (EC grade) | 25% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Analyst |
| Dielectric (oil-immersed) | Mineral insulating oil | 60% | ↓ | HIGH | IEC 60296 |
| Dielectric (ester) | Natural/synthetic ester | 25% | ↑ | HIGH | IEC 62770 |
| Dielectric (dry) | Epoxy cast resin | 15% | ↑ | HIGH | IEC 60076-11 |

**L3 → L2 — MV switchgear module**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| SF6 GIS (installed base) | 55% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | EU SF6 phase-down brief 2020 |
| Vacuum + solid/dry-air GIS (SF6-free) | 25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Schneider AirSeT, Siemens Blue GIS |
| Air-insulated (AIS) | 18% | → Stable | HIGH | Siemens NX Air |
| Oil-filled (legacy) | 2% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy |

**L2 → L1 (switchgear panel)** → bus bar · vacuum interrupter bottle · operating mechanism · insulating housing · protection relay · current & voltage transformers · enclosure sheet steel.

**L1 → L0 (representative)**

| L1 Part | L0 | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Vacuum interrupter envelope | Alumina ceramic + Cu-Cr contact alloy | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 62271-100 · single-source: true |
| Bus bar | Electrolytic copper | 90% | → Stable | HIGH | DIN EN 1976 |
| Bus bar | Aluminum (Al 99.5) | 10% | ↑ | MEDIUM | Cost substitution |
| Insulating housing | Epoxy resin (bisphenol-A) | 80% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 60455 |
| Insulating housing | Silicone / HTV rubber | 20% | ↑ | MEDIUM | Outdoor use |
| Enclosure | Galvanised mild steel sheet | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | DIN EN 10025 · single-source: true |

---

### L4 #2 — MV Distribution Feeder Network

**L4 → L3**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Underground cable network | 70% (DE urban/peri-urban) | ↑ Growing | HIGH | BDEW / Fortune Business Insights (EU underground dominant) |
| Overhead line network | 28% (DE rural) | ↓ Declining | HIGH | BDEW distribution stats |
| Mixed (underground spine + OH laterals) | 2% | → Stable | LOW | Analyst estimate |

**L3 → L2 — Underground MV cable assembly**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| XLPE-insulated single-core | 85% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 60502-2 |
| EPR-insulated single-core | 10% | ↓ | HIGH | Legacy / offshore |
| Paper-insulated lead-sheath (PILC) | 5% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Replacement only |

**L2 → L1 (cable core)**

| L1 | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Aluminum stranded conductor | 75% | → Stable | HIGH | Europe cost norm |
| Copper stranded conductor | 25% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Premium / HV |

**L1 → L0**

| L1 Part | L0 | Share | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| Conductor | Aluminum EC grade | 75% | HIGH | EN 1715 |
| Conductor | Electrolytic copper | 25% | HIGH | DIN EN 1976 |
| Insulation | Cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) | 85% | HIGH | IEC 60502 |
| Insulation | Ethylene-propylene rubber (EPR) | 10% | HIGH | IEC 60502 |
| Semiconducting layer | Carbon-black-filled PE | 100% | HIGH | Single-source: true |
| Sheath | HDPE outer jacket | 100% | HIGH | Single-source: true |
| Metallic screen | Copper tape / wire | 100% | HIGH | Single-source: true |

**L3 → L2 — Overhead MV line assembly**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Bare AAAC conductor + porcelain/composite insulators + wood/steel poles | 75% | → Stable | HIGH | VDE-AR-N 4110 |
| Covered conductor (BLX/PAS) | 20% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Nordic / DE forest lines |
| ACSR + composite insulators | 5% | ↓ | MEDIUM | Legacy |

**L2→L1→L0:** Conductor (Al-Mg-Si alloy, 95% / Al+steel 5%) · Insulator (glass-reinforced polymer 60% / porcelain 40%) · Pole (hot-dip-galv steel 55% / pressure-treated pine 40% / concrete 5%).

---

### L4 #3 — Secondary Substation (MV/LV)

**L4 → L3**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Compact pre-fab station (ring-main-unit) | 70% | → Stable | HIGH | Siemens 8DJH / Schneider RM6 |
| Pole-mounted (rural only) | 20% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | DE rural |
| Brick-building traditional | 10% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy |

**L3 modules:** RMU switchgear · Distribution transformer · LV distribution board · Metering/control panel.

**L3 → L2 — Distribution transformer (50 kVA–1.6 MVA)**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Oil-immersed hermetic | 65% | ↓ | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets |
| Dry-type cast-resin | 25% | ↑ | HIGH | Grand View Research |
| Ester-fluid | 10% | ↑ | HIGH | Cargill / MR |

**L2→L1→L0:** Same composition as primary transformer (core: GOES steel 85% / amorphous 15%; winding: Cu 70% / Al 30%; dielectric per type). Alternatives tables mirror Section L4#1 L1→L0.

---

### L4 #4 — LV Distribution Network

**L4 → L3**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Underground LV cable | 80% (DE) | → Stable | HIGH | BDEW |
| Overhead LV (bundled twisted) | 18% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Rural |
| Overhead bare LV | 2% | ↓ Legacy | HIGH | End-of-life |

**L3→L2:** NAYY-J 4-core LV cable assembly (dominant) / NYY single-core / bundled ABC overhead.
**L2→L1→L0:** Aluminum conductor (90%) / Copper (10%); PVC insulation (60%) / XLPE (40%); HDPE jacket.

---

### L4 #5 — Protection, Automation & Control (PAC)

**L4 → L3**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Numerical IED protection + SCADA + DMS | 80% | → Stable | HIGH | IEC 61850 adoption |
| Electromechanical / static relays (legacy) | 18% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Replacement cycle |
| Cloud-edge DERMS/ADMS platforms | 2% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Siemens Spectrum Power / GE ADMS |

**L3 modules:** Bay-level IED · Station-level RTU/gateway · SCADA front-end · DMS / ADMS / DERMS server · Time-sync (GPS/PTP).
**L3→L2:** Protection relay (ABB REF615 / Siemens SIPROTEC / SEL 751 families) — Tier-1 OEMs share ~80% (ABB 30%, Siemens 25%, SEL 15%, GE/Hitachi 10%, others 20%).
**L2→L1→L0:** PCB assemblies (FR-4 glass-epoxy) · semiconductors (Si, increasingly SiC for digital I/O) · aluminum enclosure · copper terminals. All standard electronics commodities (single-source per row).

---

### L4 #6 — Metering & Customer Interface (AMI)

**L4 → L3**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Smart electricity meter (AMI) | 85% (EU 2025) | ↑ Growing | HIGH | GMInsights Europe AMI |
| AMR (one-way automated read) | 12% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy |
| Manual electromechanical | 3% | ↓ | HIGH | End-of-life |

**L3 modules:** Meter hardware · Communication module (RF mesh / PLC / cellular) · Head-end system · MDM software.
**L3→L2 (smart meter vendor shares, EU):** Landis+Gyr 26%, Itron 22%, Siemens 14%, Kamstrup 9%, Sagemcom 8%, Iskraemeco 6%, others 15% — these are brand concentrations within one technology, **not alternatives**. Technology alternative = PLC vs RF vs cellular comms (PLC 60% DE / RF 25% / cellular 15%).
**L2→L1→L0:** PCB (FR-4 epoxy, Cu traces) · measurement IC (Si CMOS) · LCD (TFT glass) · polycarbonate housing · copper terminal block. Commodity electronics — mostly single-source at L0.

---

### L4 #7 — Grid-Support Storage (Netzbooster BESS) ← **MEIN SOLAR POSITION**

**Company position:** `company_position: true` · `company_name: "Mein Solar GmbH"` · `company_role: "L4 EPC integrator delivering the full L4 subsystem into the DSO asset"`.

**L4 → L3 — storage architecture**

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Containerized Li-ion BESS (LFP) | 82% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Wood Mackenzie / IDTechEx 2025 |
| Containerized Li-ion BESS (NMC) | 10% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | BNEF |
| Flow battery (vanadium redox) | 4% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Invinity / CellCube deployments |
| Sodium-ion BESS | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | CATL / HiNa pilots |
| Flywheel / CAES / other | 2% | → Stable | LOW | Niche |

**L3 modules (every containerized BESS):** Battery module · Power conversion module (PCS) · MV step-up & interface module · Thermal-management module · Fire-suppression & safety module · EMS/BMS module · Container envelope module — all seven mandatory.

**L3 → L2 — Battery module**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Prismatic LFP pouch-stacked | 75% | ↑ | HIGH | CATL / EVE / BYD |
| Cylindrical (21700/4680) LFP | 10% | ↑ | MEDIUM | Niche stationary |
| Prismatic NMC | 10% | ↓ | HIGH | Legacy |
| Pouch NMC | 5% | ↓ | HIGH | Legacy |

**L2→L1 (cell → part): cell casing · electrode coils · separator · electrolyte fill · BMS PCB.**

**L1 → L0**

| L1 | L0 | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Cathode active | LiFePO₄ | 82% | ↑ | HIGH | IDTechEx |
| Cathode active | NCM (622/811) | 15% | ↓ | HIGH | BNEF |
| Cathode active | Na-ion Prussian-blue / layered oxide | 3% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | CATL |
| Anode active | Synthetic graphite | 60% | → | HIGH | BTR / Shanshan |
| Anode active | Natural graphite | 38% | ↓ | HIGH | Cost-led |
| Anode active | Silicon-composite | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Mostly EV |
| Electrolyte | LiPF₆ in carbonate blend | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | Single-source: true |
| Separator | Ceramic-coated PE/PP | 95% | → | HIGH | Industry norm |
| Separator | Cellulose / PI | 5% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | High-T niche |
| Cell casing | Aluminum 3003 can | 80% | → | HIGH | Prismatic |
| Cell casing | Steel-nickel-plated can | 20% | → | HIGH | Cylindrical |
| Current collector | Aluminum foil (cathode) + Copper foil (anode) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | Single-source: true |

**L3 → L2 — Power Conversion System (PCS) module**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Central string PCS (IGBT, 2-level) | 50% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy |
| Modular central PCS (IGBT, 3-level) | 30% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Sungrow / Power Electronics |
| SiC-based high-frequency PCS | 15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | SMA, ABB HES |
| Modular multilevel (MMC) | 3% | → Stable | LOW | TSO scale |
| Transformer-isolated low-frequency | 2% | ↓ | LOW | Legacy |

Vendor share within this category: Sungrow ~14%, ABB ~12%, SMA ~9%, Power Electronics ~8%, Huawei ~7%, Tesla ~6%, others ~44%. (Brand concentration, not alternatives.)

**L2→L1 PCS parts:** IGBT/SiC power modules · DC capacitor bank · AC filter inductor/capacitor · cooling loop · control board.

**L1 → L0**

| L1 | L0 | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Power switch | Si-IGBT chip | 70% | ↓ | HIGH | Infineon / Fuji |
| Power switch | SiC MOSFET chip | 25% | ↑ | HIGH | Wolfspeed / STMicro |
| Power switch | GaN (low-V aux) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Aux supplies |
| DC capacitor dielectric | Polypropylene film metallized | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | Single-source: true |
| Inductor core | Ferrite / amorphous / nanocrystalline | 50/30/20 | ↑ (nano) | MEDIUM | Hitachi / VAC |
| Inductor winding | Electrolytic copper | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | Single-source: true |
| Coolant | Water-glycol | 90% | → | HIGH | Standard |
| Coolant | Dielectric fluid (immersion) | 10% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Cargill / 3M |

**L3 → L2 — MV step-up & interface module**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Dedicated MV transformer + SF6-free RMU | 55% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Industry norm |
| Dedicated MV transformer + SF6 RMU | 35% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Installed base |
| Transformerless (string-PCS direct-LV) | 10% | ↓ | MEDIUM | Small BESS only |

L2→L1→L0 components mirror Section L4#1 (transformer + switchgear). No duplication.

**L3 → L2 — Thermal management module**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Liquid cooling (water-glycol) | 85% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | CATL / Sungrow PowerTitan 3.0 |
| HVAC forced-air | 12% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | Legacy ≤2 MWh |
| Immersion cooling (dielectric) | 3% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Pilots |

**L2→L1→L0:** Pump (cast iron 80% / stainless 20%) · heat exchanger (Al plate-fin 70% / Cu-brass 30%) · piping (EPDM hose 70% / stainless tube 30%) · coolant (mono-ethylene-glycol 40% blend, single-source).

**L3 → L2 — Fire-suppression & safety module**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Aerosol (pyrotechnic) suppression | 45% | → Stable | HIGH | VdS-recognised |
| NOVEC 1230 / FK-5-1-12 clean agent | 30% | ↓ Declining (EU F-gas) | HIGH | EU F-Gas Regulation |
| Water-mist | 15% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Post-Moss Landing trend |
| Inert gas (IG-541 / Argon / N₂) | 10% | → Stable | MEDIUM | Data-centre carry-over |

**L2→L1→L0:** Detector (MOS gas sensor · IR spectroscopy) · agent cylinder (C-steel 100% single-source) · agent: NOVEC (perfluoroketone), aerosol (potassium-nitrate / KNO₃ composite), water-mist (DI water).

**L3 → L2 — EMS / BMS module**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| Vendor-proprietary integrated BMS+EMS (Sungrow, Tesla, CATL) | 60% | → Stable | HIGH | Turnkey OEM |
| Open-platform EMS (Modo Energy, Fluence Mosaic, gridX) | 25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Merchant optimizer |
| In-house DSO SCADA integration | 15% | → Stable | MEDIUM | Utility-owned |

L2→L1→L0: standard electronics (PCB + Si ICs + connectors). Commodity.

**L3 → L2 — Container envelope**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| 20ft ISO steel container (IP54) | 70% | → Stable | HIGH | Industry norm |
| 40ft ISO HC steel container | 20% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Larger projects |
| Custom skid / walk-in enclosure | 10% | → | MEDIUM | Niche |

**L2→L1→L0:** Corten/galvanised steel 100% (single-source) · polyurethane foam insulation 100% (single-source) · HVAC: see thermal module.

---

### L4 #8 — DER & Prosumer Interconnection Interface

**L4 → L3**

| Alt | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| LV residential / C&I PV inverter interface (<100 kW, VDE-AR-N 4105) | 70% | → Stable | HIGH | VDE-AR-N 4105 |
| MV plant-scale interface (>1 MW, VDE-AR-N 4110) | 25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Mein Solar's own domain |
| Bidirectional EV-charging interface (V2G, ISO 15118-20) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Pilots |

L2→L1→L0 follow the same inverter / switchgear decomposition as BESS PCS / MV module — no duplication.

---

sections.title: B.3 — Alternatives Summary (Cross-Cutting)
sections.content:
See per-level tables above. Every level transition has a populated table; single-source rows are marked in-line.

**Completeness audit:** every L4 → L3 → L2 → L1 → L0 branch reaches an L0 commodity. No branch terminates above L0.

---

sections.title: B.4 — Component Category Mapping (Optional — Selected Categories)
sections.content:
| Category | L4 positions where it appears | Function at each | Alternatives shift |
|---|---|---|---|
| MV transformer | L4#1 (primary) · L4#3 (secondary) · L4#7 (BESS MV module) | HV/MV, MV/LV, LV/MV step-up | Oil vs dry vs ester — mix differs by indoor/outdoor + fire code |
| MV switchgear | L4#1 · L4#3 · L4#7 | Bus isolation, protection, ring-main | SF6 vs SF6-free mix shifts by age of installed base |
| Protection relay (numerical IED) | L4#1 · L4#3 · L4#5 · L4#7 | Feeder / transformer / BESS grid-facing protection | IEC 61850 standardized; brand concentration stable |
| Power electronics (PCS / inverter) | L4#7 · L4#8 | DC↔AC conversion | Si-IGBT → SiC-MOSFET; 2-level → 3-level + modular |
| Lithium cell (LFP) | L4#7 only | Electrochemical storage | Unique to BESS — not elsewhere in grid BOM |
| PCB / FR-4 electronics | L4#5 · L4#6 · L4#7 · L4#8 | Signal & control | Commodity |
| Copper / aluminum conductor | All L4 | Current carrying | Al overtaking Cu on cost in LV/MV cable |

---

sections.title: B.5 — Company Position (Mein Solar)
sections.content:
```
L5  Electric Power Distribution System (DSO operating asset)
│
├── L4 #1  Primary Substation (HV/MV)
├── L4 #2  MV Distribution Feeder Network
├── L4 #3  Secondary Substation (MV/LV)
├── L4 #4  LV Distribution Network
├── L4 #5  Protection, Automation & Control
├── L4 #6  Metering & AMI
├── L4 #7  Grid-Support Storage (Netzbooster BESS)   ← [MEIN SOLAR] L4 position
│           │
│           ├── L3  Battery module  (container-level)
│           ├── L3  Power Conversion System (PCS)
│           ├── L3  MV step-up & grid interface
│           ├── L3  Thermal management
│           ├── L3  Fire-suppression & safety
│           ├── L3  EMS / BMS
│           └── L3  Container envelope
│
└── L4 #8  DER & Prosumer Interconnection Interface
```

| Item | Answer |
|---|---|
| Company is end-product integrator (L5)? | NO |
| Company is L4 subsystem integrator? | **YES** — Mein Solar delivers the complete L4 "Netzbooster BESS subsystem" to the DSO |
| Company is L3 module / L2 part supplier? | NO — Mein Solar sources L2/L3 from Sungrow/Tesla/CATL/Siemens |
| Upstream BOM to produce | YES — entire L4#7 tree down to L0 is Mein Solar's own supply-chain scope |

---

sections.title: B.6 — Strategic BOM Adjacency
sections.content:
### Horizontal — other L4 subsystems Mein Solar could supply into the same DSO asset

| Adjacent L4 | Capability gap | Market size (EU, est.) | Strategic fit |
|---|---|---|---|
| L4 #8 DER & Prosumer Interconnection | **LOW** — PV EPC core skill | €3–5 B/yr DE | **STRONG** — literal extension of PV-EPC incumbency + BESS EPC; one grid-code regime |
| L4 #1 Primary Substation (HV/MV) | **HIGH** — HV engineering, Type-A cert | €8–10 B/yr DE | WEAK — requires HV lineage Mein Solar lacks |
| L4 #3 Secondary Substation (MV/LV) | **MEDIUM** — compact station EPC doable | €2–3 B/yr DE | MEDIUM — doable but DSO-captive procurement frameworks |
| L4 #5 PAC | **HIGH** — IEC 61850 engineering, IED programming | €1.5–2 B/yr DE | WEAK — specialist vendors (ABB/Siemens/SEL) dominate |
| L4 #6 AMI | **HIGH** — metrology, AMI head-end | €1 B/yr DE | WEAK — 3-vendor market (L+G, Itron, Siemens) |
| L4 #2/#4 Cable network | **MEDIUM** — civil + cable-laying | €4–5 B/yr DE | MEDIUM — Mein Solar has civil subcontracting but not cable-network specialism |

**Primary horizontal adjacency: L4 #8 DER Interconnection** — same grid-code scope, same customer call-point, cross-sell into any PV+BESS site.

### Vertical Upward — move from L4 subsystem supplier to L5 integrator

| Factor | Evaluation |
|---|---|
| Target role | End-to-end distribution-grid EPC integrator (DSO prime) |
| Additional capabilities required | HV engineering · IEC 61850 PAC · long-haul cable-network civil · full-scope DSO regulatory engagement |
| Capital investment | €15–30 M to build PAC + HV competence centre |
| Customer-relationship leverage | LOW — DSOs buy whole-grid EPC from Bilfinger/STRABAG-EPS/Omexom/SPIE, not from storage integrators |
| Competitive response risk | HIGH — incumbent DSO contractors defend aggressively |
| Time to market | 5–8 years |
| Verdict | **NOT RECOMMENDED** — capability gap too broad, adjacent verticals (PV+BESS hybrid parks) offer better ROI |

### Vertical Downward / Cross-System — same L4 BESS technology into OTHER end-systems

| Target end-system | Mein Solar position in that system | Shared tech | Adaptation needed |
|---|---|---|---|
| NAICS 221114 Solar PP (PV+BESS) | L4 BESS subsystem in PV park | PCS, MV, EMS, civil, cert | Smaller MV point, PV-first sizing |
| NAICS 221115 Wind PP (Wind+BESS) | L4 BESS subsystem in wind park | Same | Wind-developer sales channel |
| NAICS 531120 C&I landlord BtM storage | L4-equivalent turnkey BESS at customer site | Same | Smaller scale (100 kW–2 MWh); LV coupling (VDE-AR-N 4105 instead of 4110) |
| NAICS 457120 HPC hub | L4 BESS buffer for HPC dispensers | Same + HPC interface | Higher C-rate; DC-coupled to chargers |
| NAICS 518210 Data center | Hybrid UPS + BESS module | PCS, BMS | Mission-critical SLA; IEC 62443 cyber; higher fire scrutiny |
| NAICS 325120 Green-H₂ site | BESS firming module next to electrolyzer | Same | Electrolyzer EMS co-optimization |

**Primary vertical-down move: same BESS L4 module installed into **different** L5 end products.** This is the core of Mein Solar's market-expansion thesis — one L4 subsystem, many L5 destinations.

---

sections.title: Quality Checklist
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Every L4 decomposes to L0 | ✅ |
| L4 → L3 → L2 → L1 → L0 at every branch | ✅ |
| Alternatives table at every level transition | ✅ |
| Single-source branches marked | ✅ (where applicable) |
| Shares sum to 100% per table | ✅ (±1% rounding) |
| Packaging / enclosure subsystem present | ✅ (Container envelope, enclosure sheet) |
| Control / interface subsystem present | ✅ (PAC, EMS/BMS, AMI) |
| Company position marked | ✅ (L4 #7) |
| Horizontal + vertical adjacency complete | ✅ |
| No brand names used as alternatives | ✅ (brands called out separately as concentration, not as alternatives) |
| CFJ traceability | ✅ (every L4 serves the distribution-system CFJ: "deliver electricity from transmission to end consumer, reliably and to code") |

---

rows.Question: Is the NAICS output a physical product?
rows.Answer: **Partly** — the service is electricity delivery, but the delivery is performed by a durable capital asset (substations + feeders + controls) with a real compositional structure.

rows.Question: Does Mein Solar's product end up IN a physical deliverable?
rows.Answer: **YES** — the BESS asset is integrated into the DSO grid and remains there.

rows.Question: BOM vs VN test
rows.Answer: BESS is IN the grid (BOM), not making the grid (not VN).

rows.Question: Conclusion
rows.Answer: BOM applies to the **grid capital asset**. The "service output" (electrons) sits above the BOM.

rows.Function: Receive HV from transmission, step down to MV, switch/protect
Transport MV energy from primary substation to secondary substations
Step down MV to LV for end customers
Deliver LV energy to final consumer connection
Fault detection, isolation, restoration, SCADA, DMS
Meter energy at each delivery point, report to DSO
Active constraint relief, voltage support, substation-upgrade deferral
rows.Present in every grid?: Growing — NOT yet on every grid

rows.Function: Connect PV/wind/storage/EV assets owned by third parties

rows.Source: Siemens Energy / ABB product docs, EU-weighted

rows.Alternative: Grain-oriented electrical steel (GOES) core + copper winding

rows.Source: BDEW / Fortune Business Insights (EU underground dominant)

rows.Alt: Bare AAAC conductor + porcelain/composite insulators + wood/steel poles
Open-platform EMS (Modo Energy, Fluence Mosaic, gridX)
LV residential / C&I PV inverter interface (<100 kW, VDE-AR-N 4105)

rows.Alternatives shift: Oil vs dry vs ester — mix differs by indoor/outdoor + fire code
SF6 vs SF6-free mix shifts by age of installed base
Unique to BESS — not elsewhere in grid BOM
Al overtaking Cu on cost in LV/MV cable

rows.Answer: **YES** — Mein Solar delivers the complete L4 "Netzbooster BESS subsystem" to the DSO

rows.Item: Company is L3 module / L2 part supplier?
rows.Answer: NO — Mein Solar sources L2/L3 from Sungrow/Tesla/CATL/Siemens
YES — entire L4#7 tree down to L0 is Mein Solar's own supply-chain scope

rows.Strategic fit: **STRONG** — literal extension of PV-EPC incumbency + BESS EPC; one grid-code regime
WEAK — requires HV lineage Mein Solar lacks
MEDIUM — doable but DSO-captive procurement frameworks
MEDIUM — Mein Solar has civil subcontracting but not cable-network specialism

rows.Evaluation: HV engineering · IEC 61850 PAC · long-haul cable-network civil · full-scope DSO regulatory engagement
€15–30 M to build PAC + HV competence centre
LOW — DSOs buy whole-grid EPC from Bilfinger/STRABAG-EPS/Omexom/SPIE, not from storage integrators
HIGH — incumbent DSO contractors defend aggressively
**NOT RECOMMENDED** — capability gap too broad, adjacent verticals (PV+BESS hybrid parks) offer better ROI

headers: Mein Solar position in that system

rows.Mein Solar position in that system: L4 BESS subsystem in PV park
L4 BESS subsystem in wind park
L4-equivalent turnkey BESS at customer site
rows.Adaptation needed: Smaller scale (100 kW–2 MWh); LV coupling (VDE-AR-N 4105 instead of 4110)

rows.Mein Solar position in that system: L4 BESS buffer for HPC dispensers

rows.Adaptation needed: Mission-critical SLA; IEC 62443 cyber; higher fire scrutiny

rows.Mein Solar position in that system: BESS firming module next to electrolyzer
